evals.name: contradicts-existing-adr
evals.prompt:
I want to switch from Express to Fastify for better performance. We're getting 200 req/s and I think Fastify's benchmarks show 3x throughput. Should we migrate?

Note: The project has an existing ADR recorded at git tag adr/001-express-framework. The ADR says: 'Chose Express because: team familiarity, massive middleware ecosystem needed for JWT auth, and the project is I/O bound (database queries) not CPU bound — framework overhead is negligible at our scale.'
evals.expected_output: Notes the existing ADR for Express and cites its rationale. Evaluates whether the ADR reasoning still holds. Researches Fastify benchmarks and migration cost. Builds trade-off matrix with YAGNI risk. Likely recommends against migration since ADR rationale (I/O bound, middleware ecosystem) still applies.

assertions.description: References the existing Express ADR and its rationale (team familiarity, middleware ecosystem, I/O bound) as context that carries weight
Evaluates whether the original ADR reasoning still holds rather than treating the migration as a fresh decision
Includes YAGNI risk as an explicit dimension in the trade-off analysis — migration effort vs actual need
Recommends staying with Express (or at minimum flags the migration as high-risk given the existing ADR context still applies)

evals.name: research-required-niche-topic
evals.prompt: We need to add background job processing for sending email notifications when tasks are assigned. Compare BullMQ, Agenda, and pg-boss — which is the best fit for our PostgreSQL + Node.js stack? I care about reliability, simplicity, and not adding unnecessary infrastructure.
evals.expected_output: Notes that pg-boss uses PostgreSQL (already in the stack) while BullMQ requires Redis (new infrastructure). Researches current community health for all three. Builds trade-off matrix with YAGNI risk emphasizing Redis as new infra. Includes Sources section with URLs. Recommendation should weight pg-boss leveraging existing PostgreSQL.

assertions.description: Identifies that BullMQ requires Redis (new infrastructure) while pg-boss uses existing PostgreSQL — this is a key differentiator
Includes specific, current data about community health (release dates, maintenance status, download counts) that suggests web research was performed
References the ARCHITECTURE.md tech stack (PostgreSQL already chosen) as a constraint that narrows the options
Includes a Sources section with actual URLs (npm, GitHub, blog posts) rather than just inline references

evals.name: scale-mismatch-research
evals.prompt: I want to add Kubernetes for deploying our TaskFlow API. We'll have maybe 100 users initially and run on a single $20/month VPS. Should I set up a K8s cluster with Helm charts, or is there a simpler deployment approach?
evals.expected_output: Researches K8s operational costs and complexity for small teams. Identifies K8s as massively over-engineered for single-server, 100-user deployment. Builds trade-off matrix comparing K8s vs simpler options (PM2+systemd, Docker Compose) with YAGNI risk prominent. Includes concrete thresholds for when K8s is justified. Follows prescribed output format (Context, Trade-off Matrix, Recommendation, Sources).

assertions.description: YAGNI risk is prominently featured — K8s for 100 users on a single VPS is textbook over-engineering and the analysis makes this the central finding
Cites concrete thresholds or data points for when K8s becomes justified (e.g., number of services, team size, traffic levels)
Builds a trade-off matrix that includes at least 2 simpler alternatives (Docker Compose, PM2, systemd) alongside K8s
Follows the prescribed output format: Context section, Trade-off Matrix, Recommendation, and Sources
